paper_title: Hash Layers For Large Sparse Models

challenges: Poor routing leading to non-specialized experts
Computational inefficiency of dense softmax approaches
Load balancing issues in existing methods

existing_approaches.name: Switch Transformers
existing_approaches.description: Uses softmax over token's hidden state but requires load balancing terms

existing_approaches.name: BASE Layers
existing_approaches.description: Employs linear assignment algorithm to address load balancing

proposed_solution.name: Hash-based routing strategy
proposed_solution.description: Tokens are routed to fixed expert modules based on their hash
advantages: No change to objective function or assignment algorithm
Simple, robust, fast, and easy to implement

results.comparisons: Compares favorably to both Switch Transformers and BASE Layers
results.applications: Advocated for use in settings with limited compute budgets where only one shot at training large models is possible

technical_details.setting: Same as prior work [11, 8, 10] where a feedforward network (FFN) in a Transformer is replaced by its MoE version
technical_details.input: Input sequences {x₁, x₂, ..., x_T} of T tokens
technical_details.processing: Representations are computed in parallel by a standard Transformer

key_figure.description: Overview of the Hash Layer showing tokens routed to fixed expert modules based on their hash

title: Hash Layers For Large Sparse Models
